It’s a bit tough to get a good look at Nykke, given how tightly she’s pressed and cuddled herself up against you, but you can’t help but want to get a better look down your Qim’s body, and to drink in what you can see.
You get a better look at Nykke, currently half-cuddled against you and lounging in the snow, overlooking the ocean.

Nykke is extremely tall, standing at ten feet tall from sole to scalp, not counting her wings. Similar to any pureblood Frostwyrm, she has six limbs, although they’re shaped quite differently: she walks on two plantigrade feet, and she has four arms: two each sprouting from her left and right shoulders. She has five toes on each foot and five fingers on each hand, although each digit is capped with a razor sharp talon, no less lethal than a Frostwyrm’s, although they’re considerably shorter.

Her body is covered in pitch-black scales that glisten darkly in the reflective light of the snow; each scale is about the size of a fingernail, and each scale is as hard as metal. Along her front, going from her lower jaw to the inside of her thighs, she has softer, yielding, malleable dark-gray scales. Although you doubt many conventional weapons would be able to wound her, she has a number of battle scars dotting her frame, from her arms to her legs – she’s lived a wild life on Uveto for over two hundred years, so it only makes sense.

She has a long, thick tail at her coccyx; it’s as thick as her waist at its trunk and it only tapers slightly at the tip, where it’s adorned with blunter spikes, better for bludgeoning than piercing. She has only one set of teeth in her mouth, but like a Frostwyrm, they are each sharp enough to rip apart any armor. Nykke is the perfect hybrid combination of the svelte, flexible, stealthy humanoid form and the power and ferocity of a Frostwyrm; there are few creatures in the universe that would be able to best her in combat, with some appropriate training.

Focusing on her gentler features, Nykke’s eyes have pink sclera, ruby-red irises and jet-black pupils. She has a humanoid face, with gentle, smooth cheeks, more similar to skin rather than scales, and an upturned, button nose and two nostrils. She has two thin, jet-black lips that can curl into the most precious – or mischievous – of smiles. Growing from her forehead are a pair of horns that curve sharply over her skull, but don’t reach very far and are blunt at the tip, useless for attacks. And beneath those, she has thin, shoulder-length, dull-blue hair.

Growing from her shoulderblades are two massive wings, with a combined wingspan of twenty feet. When they’re folded behind her, they still reach behind her by about three additional feet, and they have a vertical reach of about eight, extending well past her head. Though they’re large and intimidating, she moves with them comfortably enough, and their thick, leathery musculature easily provides more than enough lift to give her flight.

Between her two powerful legs are a pair of testicles, wrapped snuggly in a taut, leathery scrotum. Each nut is about the size of your clenched fist. Above them is a penile vent; unlike with [frostwyrm.name], it bulges rather obviously with the girth of the penis inside it, even when completely unaroused, acting more like a sheath than a vent. When fully aroused, her penis extends by about a foot from her body, with a girth as thick as your forearm. It’s covered from root to crown with pulsing blue veins, and it has several nubs and pricks all along its underside. The tip is flat and mostly featureless, save for a rim of muscle around its glans, reminding you of a tree trunk. At the base of her penis is a thick knot, bloating her already-impressive size all the larger, to properly tie and breed with whoever her partner is.

Concealed behind her testicles is her vagina: the exposed labia are bluish-pink, but it otherwise looks very similar to a vagina of your own kind. It’s appropriately sized for her form, although her body is far more elastic than it appears: it can easily take almost any insertion, including from a pure-blooded Frostwyrm, despite them being twice her size. Unlike [frostwyrm.name], she has a clitoris, extending about an inch or two when fully aroused.
Though it’s usually concealed by her tail, she has a large, blue-ringed anus where her tail meets her spine, right where it belongs.

You ask Nykke if she’s willing to have a chat with you. Surely she has a distinct perspective on some topics that she, as a Uvetan native, would have compared to yours.
“Yeah, I don’t mind having a chat,” she says. “Maybe it’ll help get my mind off some... other topics.”

As subtle as a brick, she uncrosses her legs, giving you an eyeful of her exposed genitals – her Frostwyrm cock poking into the cold breeze of the air, accentuated by a pair of thick, low-hanging, full balls – and crosses them again, the other leg on top of the other.
Great, now you hope the conversation will take your mind off other things, too.

You ask Nykke if she’s put any thought into what sort of person she’d want in a mate. If [frostwyrm.name] and her example is anything to go by, Frostwyrms aren’t a disparaging species; being a Frostwyrm is more of a bonus than a necessity, and each Frostwyrm has a different idea of what makes an ideal mate.

“Uh... I can’t say I have, I guess,” she says, her two left index fingers tapping on her chin in thought. “I’ve had so many ideas over the Sky Cycles that they’ve all kind of meshed together over time. I guess my first priority would be a mate that can give me an ideally endless number of kips. I’m sure your own Qim wouldn’t disagree.”

You like to think that that’s kind of a blasé way of describing your relationship with [frostwyrm.name]. At the word, you feel Nykke’s psionics probe your mind – she isn’t quite as quick or refined on her skills, and it takes her a moment to understand the definition of the word ‘blasé.’ You tell her that you and [frostwyrm.name] having lots of offspring is just one of the many qualities you fulfill as her mate.
“How did your Qim decide for you to be her mate?” she asks. “I could look in your memories and see for myself, but I understand that non-Frostwyrms don’t like that so much.”

You explain that you two met in combat. Nykke hums – she gathered that much at first. It must not surprise her that there can be a bit of a ‘ritual’ when Frostwyrms find a potential mate. You regale that you unknowingly wandered into her territory once, and she mistook that you were trying to invade her space. You never actually asked [frostwyrm.name] why she attacked you; it seems to unimportant, now.

Anyway, you bested [frostwyrm.name] in combat. “You, really?” Nykke asks in bewilderment. You answer that you had done it several times, not just once. “But you’re so much smaller than her! And her teeth – how did you avoid getting eaten in one big–”

She finishes the sentence by leaning at you and loudly chomping at the air between you, showing off her own pointed teeth. You insist that it’s true; maybe one day she can ask [frostwyrm.name] herself. There’s more to combat than whether one kind is smaller than another, or whether one kind has larger, sharper teeth and claws. You can go into deeper detail later, if she wants.

In any case, [frostwyrm.name] had established her territory along a route that you regularly traveled, meaning you and she were at odds a number of times. And every single time you won, you let her go, because you didn’t want the blood of something as beautiful as herself on your hands.

But, one day, [frostwyrm.name] managed to get the better of you. And when she had the upper-hand and was in a position to kill you – she didn’t. She recognized you and the fact that you kept sparing her, and she extended the same mercy to you.

Nykke, despite her halfway-bored expression telling you she’s only modestly interested in your story, is hanging on your every word, her bright red eyes impatiently watching yours every time you pause for effect. To finish your story, you tell her that [frostwyrm.name] recognized your strength, and pined that she hadn’t found another pureblood Frostwyrm like herself, so she decided that you were worthy to breed with her.

“Hmm,” Nykke hums, processing your story. You can feel her large heart beat through her chest in excitement – she must like the idea that, if a pureblood Frostwyrm like [frostwyrm.name] would accept you, then she isn’t in the wrong for looking for non-Frostwyrms as well. You ask her if she has any ideas on what her mate should be.

“Well, the strength to stand next to a Frostwyrm, for sure,” she says, hugging your form, unsubtly testing how taut your body is. “A proper mate must be able to defend their dwelling and protect their clutch, and if you had the strength to fell someone like your Qim so many times over, I wouldn’t settle for anyone lesser.”

You thank her for the compliment, but tell her that you’re not nearly the strongest of your own kind. Like you had said earlier, there’s more to combat than just bulk. “Did you outsmart her, then?” Nykke asks, and you say that, in a manner of speaking, you certainly used the right tools for the job. “And you’re so young compared to us! A proper mate should be cunning and intelligent enough to stand with a Frostwyrm, as well.”

You implore her to continue. “A good, proper mate would show compassion, like you had shown to your Qim. You didn’t kill her, even when you could, and when she asked you to help her repopulate her species, you didn’t hesitate. It reminds me of my own Qal and how he met my Qim. A good mate would know when to protect a kind that needs help, and when a battle is over.”

Nykke breathes sharply through her nose, the air hissing into her nostrils. “And a good mate,” she continues, her six limbs constricting you tighter as her fantasies start to get away from her, her fingers getting a little exploratory, “would be close with every single one of their many, many kips, and spend lots of time with them. They’d ensure that their kips grew up strong enough, and wise enough, to stand with a proper Frostwyrm. And a proper mate would ensure they have a strong sense of the world around them. And... a good mate would never shy away from making more kips with their mate. And a good mate–”

You interrupt Nykke and tell her – with her bright-red eyes locked squarely onto yours, her breath shallow, her fingers still rubbing at you, and her nostrils flaring, likely smelling [frostwyrm.name] on you and driving her wild – that you have a pretty close idea of what, or who, she would consider to be a viable mate.

“Then enough with the games, already,” she begs, her voice lilting. It sounded like a particularly... vulnerable request: if you had a stronger psionic link with her, you don’t doubt you’d be inundated with lust, and you’re not sure if you’d be able to restrain yourself as much as she must be now. “It’s torture sitting next to such a viable mate and they won’t
let me get started on a massive clutch with them!
let me put a kip in them!
put a kip in me!
Nykke, as a Frostwyrm herself, knows better than anyone that Frostwyrms mate monogamously. Starting a second family altogether would be taboo among her culture.
She huffs and bites into her lower lip, her eyes scanning the ocean without another word.
But, you continue, if she can be a little more patient... your kind has invented a way to mate without it involving conception, if that’ll at least help her with her urges.
“Why in the world would your kind invent that?” she asks, bewildered. “What’s the point of mating if you don’t want kips?”
For the pleasure of it, you answer matter-of-factly.
She crosses her legs again. You catch the distinct whiff of her musk in the salty oceanic air as she does so. “Fine,” she says. “I’ll wait a bit more.”

You ask Nykke how her handle on her psionic strengths are. If she’s a Frostwyrm, and she’s as old as [frostwyrm.name], she must have a strong handle on them by now.
The question is met with a quizzical pause. “I’m... not sure what you’re asking me,” she admits.

You recall that [frostwyrm.name] didn’t have the same approach to psionics as you did, either. In her own words, what you call ‘psionics’ is the skill that Frostwyrms have that allow them to communicate without using words, like you are now.

Oh, like this? Nykke asks, her voice projecting into your mind directly. You concur: exactly like that. [frostwyrm.name] also said that Frostwyrms don’t necessarily communicate in words: they communicate in ideas and emotions, and are able to project feelings directly into whoever they’re connected with. You’re still new to the whole thing, yourself.

My own Qal had the same reaction to our ability to communicate. I thought he was just incapable, for one reason or another, and I didn’t believe him when he said that communicating like this was a unique skill. Nykke focuses her gaze on you, and you can tell that she’s trying to ‘impress’ something into your psyche – like an idea, or an emotion – but all you wind up feeling is a dull thud in the front of your head. Do you feel anything?

You answer that you don’t. She grunts out loud and stops her attempts. “Talking with our mouths is so awkward!” she curses to the ocean. “And using ‘words?’ What’s the point of communicating at all if you’re going to hide your intentions behind ‘words?’”

She turns to you, her eyes arching and her lips flat, trying to mask her expression as best as she could. “You know, I never questioned my Qim’s affection for my Qal. I could feel it whenever they spoke of him. But neither of us could feel it from him back. He always told us that he’d go to the ends of the ‘universe,’ whatever that was, for us. But we never felt his sincerity.” Her expression quickly sours, as if you had just personally wronged her. “What if your ‘words’ could be used to say something you don’t feel? How do I know that every single word you’ve ever said didn’t truly mean what you felt?”

Funny enough, there’s a word in your language for that exact sort of question: you tell Nykke that being unable to inherently trust anyone at their word, and assuming that everyone is a liar because they have the potential to be a liar, is called ‘paranoia.’ It’s true, you can’t convey your emotions as well as she or [frostwyrm.name] can to you, but that doesn’t make you untrustworthy. [frostwyrm.name] relies on you despite of that, and... well, there isn’t a word in your language that can describe how that makes you feel about her.

You consider Nykke and [frostwyrm.name] to be gifted and privileged; being able to communicate to each other so purely is a real gift that should be shared with the world, if they could. There are an untold number of languages out there: if everyone could skip the words and jump straight to the ideas, a lot of barriers would be taken down in a hurry.

Nykke’s eyes stay on yours, and you unflinchingly stare back, hoping that she’ll find your earnestness in them. Apparently satisfied, she relaxes back into her snowy seat, her tail wagging once beneath her, content in what she found. Do you think you could learn? To communicate like my kind could?
[frostwyrm.name] had put it best, when you asked her. In her own words, you are as equally equipped as any Frostwyrm; [frostwyrm.name] doesn’t seem to think you can’t be taught.
I could try to teach you, she offers. It catches you by surprise – you hadn’t expected Nykke to be so accommodating.

You reply that you’re supposed to be the Qal, and from what you’ve learned, that means you’re supposed to be the teacher. You’d suppose that, if Nykke went through the effort to teach you to communicate like a Frostwyrm, that would make her the Qal between you two.

“Hmmph,” Nykke hums, her hips squirming in the snow beneath her. “That sounds like a great idea to me. Maybe we should skip the lessons.” Her eyes are on you again, narrowed predatorily, and her lips split apart by her tongue, wetting them in a long, languid swipe. “I’m giving you one chance to back out.”
You laugh, and say that you’ll take the lessons. Maybe you can save the happy ending for whether it works out or not.

The next hour or so between you two is spent with Nykke trying to put to words what she does when she uses her psionic focus. What she concentrates on, and how she can filter one thought from another – it’s a far more involved process than you initially thought. The mind takes in so much information so quickly, all while coming up with its own thoughts, ideas, and expressions, that if Nykke weren’t careful and selective and simply let the whole gamut of thoughts across the link, you’d easily confuse yourself for her.
However, despite your best effort to learn, and Nykke’s best effort to teach, you ultimately don’t come any closer to understanding how psionics work, much less mastering them.

You have a link to [frostwyrm.name], and a minor one to Nykke, but while you are able to receive any thought, idea, expression, or inspiration, it’s a one-way connection for you, and you aren’t able to communicate your own thoughts back across it. Whenever you have a thought, Nykke is able to pick up on it and understand it, but that’s not the same as you projecting it to her.

“Are there other kinds in the world that can communicate the way my kind can?” Nykke asks, exasperated from an hour’s worth of diligence with nothing to show for it. “If you can’t do it, and my Qal couldn’t do it, it’s hard not to think that us Frostwyrms might be alone.”
You respond that, as far as you know, Frostwyrms are one of the only kinds that can. Maybe it’s simply a uniquely-Uvetan trait.

“No, I don’t agree with that,” Nykke says with some finality, leaning into you, her eyes narrowing determinedly on yours. “I understand what your Qim meant: we’re equally equipped. You should be able to communicate like I can. It’s just....”
It’s just that you can’t?

“I don’t know how to explain it. Argh, this is all very frustrating!” she exclaims to the ocean. “It’s not like it’s some secret. I understand what I’m doing; any of my kind would understand if I asked them. But being unable to explain it in a way that another kind that can understand–” In her frustration, she picks up two fistfuls of snow with both her left hands, and hurls them as hard as she can into the water. “‘Words’ were a mistake!”

You lean into Nykke, snuggling yourself against her enormous form to get her to calm down. You thank her for going through the effort for you, and you understand that it’s not easy to do. You liken it to explaining what the color blue is to anyone that hasn’t seen it before.

Nykke probes your mind, in an effort to understand what you mean, but she isn’t in there for long. “Yes! Exactly like that!” she says, clapping herself on the leg, excited that she can at least vocalize her frustrations. “How in the Grand Qim’s shadow do you explain something so fundamental to someone?” She leans into you, reciprocating the snuggle as she tries to calm herself down. “At least your kind knows how to communicate such frustrations.”
There, you see? Words aren’t all bad.
That gets her to laugh, which is good: you’ve gotten her to calm down a bit. You suggest that you two can try again later, if she’d like.
She ‘tch’s’ at the suggestion, but doesn’t immediately dismiss it. “Maybe in a Light’s Rise or two,” she says. “It’ll give me some time to think about it.”
Psionics

Out of turn, you ask Nykke what she knows about the world.

She has to think about her answer for a moment: she taps at her chin and roams her eyes across the ocean’s horizon, considering how to formulate her response. “I’m told that it’s very large,” she says, after a moment. “I had been wandering ever since I left my Qim’s lair. If the world has an end to it, I’m not sure if I’ll ever find it. Although there are fewer mates in the world than I was expecting to find.”

You tell Nykke that you aren’t a Frostwyrm, or, at least, you weren’t always; you were born as [pc.aOriginalRace]. You ask her to guess how many of your kind exist. “Hundreds?” she asks, and you tell her there are more. “Thousands?” More than that. Her eyes squint in concentration. “Hmm....”

You tell Nykke that you aren’t a Frostwyrm, you’re a [pc.race]. You ask her to guess how many of your kind exist. “Hundreds?” she asks, and you tell her there are more. “Thousands?” More than that. Her eyes squint in concentration. “Hmm....”
There are more of your own kind than she might be able to properly envision: a number so large that she might not be able to understand the magnitude of how many exist.

And, on top of there being so many of your own kind, there are other worlds out there. Your kind have named the world as Nykke knows it as ‘Uveto;’ you’ve been to so many other planets and interacted with so many other kinds by now, that you’d have a hard time categorizing all of it.

You start simple: you ask Nykke if she knows what it means to be warm. “I do,” she says, and you can tell that she’s relieved that you’re finally using words she understands. “‘Warm’ is what I would feel when I curled up against my Qal.” She leans into you, her two, thick right arms curling around you and pulling her into her muscular frame. “Although I’m sure you can find a way to make me warmer than that, in all your wisdom, [pc.name].”
You tell her that it’s something you’ll consider, but right now, you’re trying to be a teacher to her. To continue, you ask Nykke if she knows what it means to be ‘hot.’

You feel the same tugging at your mind, but ask her to answer in her own words. “Uh...,” she stutters. “No.” You teach her that, basically, ‘hot’ is what happens when you’re too warm. “Too warm? There’s no such thing, is there?” There is, yes: when you’re too warm, it feels like you’re suffocating, and that you’re trapped in your own body. If the air around you is too warm, it hurts to breath; if the ground beneath you is too warm, it hurts to walk. When the warmth is all-pervasive, you can feel like there’s just... nothing you can do to escape it.

You ask her if she’s not reading your mind. She’s normally the type of Frostwyrm to skip the words and jump straight to the psionics, leaving you at a bit of a disadvantage.

“I guess [frostwyrm.name] is rubbing off on me a bit,” she responds with a half-hearted shrug. “She doesn’t like it when I comm – uh, when I ‘read your mind’ without your permission.” She flaps her lips a bit, sticking her tongue out from between them. “And as much as I prefer to communicate with our emotions, I... well, I figured that it might be unfair to you, since you can’t communicate like we can, and if you’re going to be my mate and my Qal, and you are my Qal,” she detours, squeezing you closer to her again, “I should probably try and do what I can to make you not feel... I don’t know... lonely? Excluded?”

That’s a sweet sentiment for her to make, that she would go through the effort of trying to make you feel more comfortable with her, and you reciprocate the squeeze, nestling yourself against her. That’s something that a proper mate would do for another.
“I’m off to a good start, then,” she muses, resting her cheek against your scalp. You two enjoy each other’s company for another, silent moment. “But seriously,” she says, “what’s got you so quiet?”

Answering honestly, you tell her that you were just reflecting on how you, her, and [frostwyrm.name] had all come to where you are now. Sometime between now and the past two-hundred years, Frostwyrms had become an endangered species, but despite that, you’re now the Qal to two of them, and they’re supposed to be monogamous. That’s a lot to accept.

At first, Nykke says nothing as she contemplates your observations. “Yeah, I guess that’s all true,” she says. “[frostwyrm.name] and I both grew up thinking that we wouldn’t have a problem finding a mate, and now, mates are in such high demand that she and I have to share one.” She turns her head, looking at the walls of her makeshift lair. “I wasn’t even able to find a proper home and I had to carve a fake one out of [frostwyrm.name]’s.”
You tentatively ask Nykke if she’s okay with how this all came to be. You know she had higher hopes for finding a mate.
She hesitates before responding. “[frostwyrm.name] said something similar, didn’t she? That you weren’t her first choice?”
You respond affirmatively. It’s a fact that [frostwyrm.name] likes to bring up more times than you’d like, to be totally honest.

“I think she likes to bring it up so often because she’s learned a lesson from it,” Nykke continues. “It’s no secret between the three of us that you weren’t what either [frostwyrm.name] or I had wanted. But you were what the Grand Qim gave us. You are everything that we could have asked for – you weren’t what we wanted in a mate, [pc.name], but you were what we needed. I understand exactly what [frostwyrm.name] means when she says she wouldn’t have chosen a different mate if she went back in time and knew what she knows now. We’re both proud to call you our Qal.”

She pulls you more tightly against you and wraps all four arms, and both her legs, around you, spooning you as deeply as she can. She doesn’t properly answer your yes-or-no question, but... this is good enough for you, really.

All that said, though, there is one question that you want to ask Nykke. A bit of a lascivious one. You ask her what her opinion is on the sex that you, her, and [frostwyrm.name] have. Frostwyrms, being monogamous, don’t exactly have a ton of experience with threesomes.

“Frankly,” she says, pulling her head away and looking into your eyes with a salaciously wry grin, “it’s not so much that [frostwyrm.name] and I have two mates, [pc.name], and it’s more about how we do it. It’s not enough for [frostwyrm.name] to stick her penis inside me; we have to do this whole ritual where you and I get underneath her and give her endowments a bath that lasts too long, or you have to fuck me from behind while my face gets pushed into her body. It’s vulgar. It’s improper. The Grand Qim would turn her white scales pink at the very idea of it.”
Yeah, [frostwyrm.name] usually said something among those lines when you introduced her to how your kind has sex. And now she can barely imagine having sex any other way.
“Yep,” Nykke agrees with a laugh, her thighs grinding together subconsciously. “And I bet that if the Grand Qim ever had a taste, they’d cum enough to fill [frostwyrm.name]’s lair knee-deep.”

Nykke stops, her eyes cast down across her body towards her thighs, each of them rubbing against your own. Her already-obscene-and-bulging penile shaft begins to split, and the musk of her cock quickly permeates the small room of her makeshift hold. “And now I’m in the mood,” she laughs again. “Be a ‘proper mate’ and do something about it, will you, my Qal?”

You ask Nykke how her handle on her psionic strengths are. If she’s a Frostwyrm, and she’s as old as [frostwyrm.name], she must have a strong handle on them by now.
The question is met with a quizzical pause. “I’m... not sure what you’re asking me,” she admits.

You recall that [frostwyrm.name] didn’t have the same approach to psionics as you did, either. In her own words, what you call ‘psionics’ is the skill that Frostwyrms have that allow them to communicate without using words, like you are now.

Oh, like this? Nykke asks, her voice projecting into your mind directly. You concur: exactly like that. [frostwyrm.name] also said that Frostwyrms don’t necessarily communicate in words: they communicate in ideas and emotions, and are able to project feelings directly into whoever they’re connected with. You’re still new to the whole thing, yourself.

My own Qal had the same reaction to our ability to communicate. I thought he was just incapable, for one reason or another, and I didn’t believe him when he said that communicating like this was a unique skill. Nykke focuses her gaze on you, and you can tell that she’s trying to ‘impress’ something into your psyche – like an idea, or an emotion – but all you wind up feeling is a dull thud in the front of your head. Do you feel anything?

You answer that you don’t. She grunts out loud and stops her attempts. “Talking with our mouths is so awkward!” she curses to the walls of her lair. “And I never liked using ‘words.’ What’s the point of communicating if you can hide your intentions behind ‘words?’ I could have introduced myself as anyone, and you wouldn’t be able to tell if it was true or not.”

Suddenly, the fight drains from her body, and she slumps against the ice wall of her miniature lair. “You know, I never questioned my Qim’s affection for my Qal. I could feel it whenever they spoke of him. But neither of us could feel it from him back. He always told us that he’d go to the ends of the ‘universe,’ whatever that was, for us. But we never felt his sincerity.” She rests her cheek against your shoulder and sighs through her nose – a long-winded, exasperated sigh, almost like she was defeated. “And I don’t want to question your affection for me, but you have to understand – when Frostwyrms like [frostwyrm.name] and myself go from feeling emotions directly to needing to put trust in something that could be untrue, it’s... it’s enough to make us wonder. We aren’t used to the very concept of insincerity.”

Funny enough, there’s a word in your language for that exact sort of sentiment: you tell Nykke that being unable to inherently trust anyone at their word, and assuming that everyone is a liar because they have the potential to be a liar, is called ‘paranoia.’ It’s true, you can’t convey your emotions as well as she or [frostwyrm.name] can to you, but that doesn’t make you untrustworthy. You know that she knows that, but sometimes, it’s better to put it to words anyway.

You consider Nykke and [frostwyrm.name] to be gifted and privileged; being able to communicate to each other so purely is a real gift that should be shared with the world, if they could. There are an untold number of languages out there: if everyone could skip the words and jump straight to the ideas, a lot of barriers would be taken down in a hurry.

Nykke believed you from the start: now that you two are officially mates, it’s not in her to distrust you for any reason, but it’s still worthwhile to her to hear things from your perspective. Her tail wags once beneath her, content in what she hears. Do you think you could learn? To communicate like my kind could?
[frostwyrm.name] had put it best, when you asked her. In her own words, you are as equally equipped as any Frostwyrm; [frostwyrm.name] doesn’t seem to think you can’t be taught.
I could try to teach you, she offers. It catches you by surprise, only because you didn’t expect her to offer.

You reply that you’re supposed to be the Qal, and from what you’ve learned, that means you’re supposed to be the teacher. You’d suppose that, if Nykke went through the effort to teach you to communicate like a Frostwyrm, that would make her the Qal between you two.

“Hmmph,” Nykke hums, her hips squirming in the snow beneath her. “That sounds like a great idea to me. Maybe we should skip the lessons.” Her eyes are on you again, narrowed predatorily, and her lips split apart by her tongue, wetting them in a long, languid swipe. “I’m giving you one chance to back out.”

You laugh, and say that you’ll take the lessons. Although, if she wants to try some role-reversal later, you suppose you’d be okay with that. You wonder if the sex will feel any different for the Qim than it does for the Qal.

The next hour or so between you two is spent with Nykke trying to put to words what she does when she uses her psionic focus. What she concentrates on, and how she can filter one thought from another – it’s a far more involved process than you initially thought. The mind takes in so much information so quickly, all while coming up with its own thoughts, ideas, and expressions, that if Nykke weren’t careful and selective and simply let the whole gamut of thoughts across the link, you’d easily confuse yourself for her.
However, despite your best effort to learn, and Nykke’s best effort to teach, you ultimately don’t come any closer to understanding how psionics work, much less mastering them.

You have a link to both [frostwyrm.name] and Nykke, but while you are able to receive any thought, idea, expression, or inspiration, it’s a one-way connection for you, and you aren’t able to communicate your own thoughts back across it. Whenever you have a thought, Nykke is able to pick up on it and understand it, but that’s not the same as you projecting it to her.

“Are there other kinds in the world that can communicate the way my kind can?” Nykke asks, exasperated from an hour’s worth of diligence with nothing to show for it. “If you can’t do it, and my Qal couldn’t do it, it’s hard not to think that us Frostwyrms might be alone.”

You respond that psionics are a well-known and documented form of communication among some known races within the universe, similar to Frostwyrms, but you’ve never heard of them communicating purely through emotion similar to how she and her kind do. Maybe their method of communication is wholly unique to them.

“No, I don’t agree with that,” Nykke says with some finality, leaning into you, her eyes narrowing determinedly on yours. “I understand what [frostwyrm.name] meant: we’re equally equipped. You should be able to communicate like I can. It’s just....”
It’s just that you can’t?

“I don’t know how to explain it. Argh, this is all very frustrating!” she exclaims, her voice echoing off the icy walls of her lair. “It’s not like it’s some secret. I understand what I’m doing; any of my kind would understand if I asked them. But being unable to explain it in a way that another kind that can understand–” She flexes all four hands in aggravation, her sharp claws jutting from each of her fingers, and she releases that pent-up stress by slamming them all into the floor beside her, driving her claws into the ice. “‘Words’ were a mistake!”

You lean into Nykke, snuggling yourself against her enormous form to get her to calm down. You thank her for going through the effort for you, and you understand that it’s not easy to do. You liken it to explain what the color blue is to anyone that hasn’t seen it before.

Nykke probes your mind, in an effort to understand what you mean, but she isn’t in there for long. “Yes! Exactly like that!” she says, clapping herself on the leg, excited that she can at least vocalize her frustrations. “How in the Grand Qim’s shadow do you explain something so fundamental to someone?” She leans into you, reciprocating the snuggle as she tries to calm herself down. “At least your kind knows how to communicate such frustrations.”
There, you see? Words aren’t all bad.
That gets her to laugh, which is good: you’ve gotten her to calm down a bit. You suggest that you two can try again later, if she’d like.
She ‘tch’s’ at the suggestion, but doesn’t immediately dismiss it. “Maybe in a Light’s Rise or two,” she says. “It’ll give me some time to think about it.”

Out of turn, you ask Nykke what she knows about the world.

She has to think about her answer for a moment: she taps at her chin and roams her eyes across her dugout lair, considering how to formulate her response. “I’m told that it’s very large,” she says, after a moment. “I had been wandering ever since I left my Qim’s lair. If the world has an end to it, I’m not sure if I’ll ever find it. But, then again, I was also told that there were many more of my kind than I found.”

original: There are more of your own kind than she might be able to properly envision: a number so large that she might not be able to understand the magnitude of how many exist.

And, on top of there being so many of your own kind, there are other worlds out there. Your kind have named the world as Nykke knows it as ‘Uveto;’ you’ve been to so many other planets and interacted with so many other kinds by now, that you’d have a hard time categorizing all of it.

You start simple: you ask Nykke if she knows what it means to be warm. “I sure do,” she says, pulling on you and squeezing you tighter against her. “‘Warm’ is what I would feel when I curled up against my Qal, just like this.” She leans into you, her two, thick right arms curling around you and pulling her into her muscular frame. “Although this feels different, somehow.”
‘I should hope so,’ you think to yourself. To continue, you ask Nykke if she knows what it means to be ‘hot.’

You feel the same tugging at your mind, but ask her to answer in her own words. “Uh...,” she stutters. “No.” You teach her that, basically, ‘hot’ is what happens when you’re too warm. “Too warm? There’s no such thing, is there?” There is, yes: when you’re too warm, it feels like you’re suffocating, and that you’re trapped in your own body. If the air around you is too warm, it hurts to breath; if the ground beneath you is too warm, it hurts to walk. When the warmth is all-pervasive, you can feel like there’s just... nothing you can do to escape it.

“But it feels that way if we’re too cold, as well,” she notes. That’s right! If it’s too cold, then it can feel just as limiting and painful. Just as there are places like Uveto that are always very cold, there can be places that are always very hot.

Nykke considers your words, but from the expression on her face and the low, contemplative growl in her throat, she’s not liking what you’re telling her. “Is there any place that is neither?” she asks; she’s finding it hard to believe that the world – or worlds – can be such difficult, unforgiving places to be. You answer that some places may be too warm, and some places may be too cold, but being able to overcome those obstacles and survive, and even thrive, in such environments is what makes kinds like your own so hardy. And, if Nykke is truly the daughter of an Ausar, she’ll be able to adapt and survive in any environment, too.

Now that you’ve properly gotten Nykke hooked on your stories, you continue enthusiastically; you start with your parentage and that you’re an heir to... well, it’d be a difficult to explain in a way she understands, but, in the very barest, lamest terms you can, you explain that your own parent had passed away some time ago, and you’re on a journey to reclaim his own lair for yourself.

“You did not stay with your Qim and your Qal until they were summoned?” Nykke asks, and you confirm; your kind typically only stay with their parents until they’re fully matured, and then they set out on their own, to find their own lairs and their own mates. “Now that you mention it,” she says, “my Qal did say something about that. He found it unusual that Frostwyrms would stay with their Qim and Qal until their summoning.”

When your own father was, as Nykke would understand it, ‘summoned,’ you had set out on a mission to claim what was his for your own. That’s taken you to planets full of lush, dense, green jungle; planets of trashy, difficult, desert terrain and wasteland; deep into dark, rocky caves; and even to very small, man-made planets called ‘stations.’ Uveto is just one of the more recent stops in your mission to claim your inheritance.

You feel something pulling at your mind; a curious, probing sensation, rummaging through your memories, almost the same way that [frostwyrm.name] plucks at your head when you use a word she doesn’t understand. As interested as Nykke is on your stories, she doesn’t understand half the things you’re saying. She probably doesn’t even know what a ‘green’ is, never mind a ‘jungle.’

Nykke’s eyes widen in her head when she finds your conjured images of what a jungle looks like, and what a station looks like. “I could never have imagined that the world would be such a diverse place. I thought that everywhere was like this,” she pauses, gesturing to the ice around you, “everywhere.” Her psionic ‘pulling’ becomes especially intense when you share the image of a cave with her – something about the darker environment has her attention. “Could I travel to any of these places as well?”

You can feel her imagination running wild in your own head: she doesn’t like the idea of being somewhere that’s too hot, or someplace that’s somehow even colder than Uveto, but she nonetheless wants to explore them for her own. You tell her that she could – there’s nothing saying she couldn’t travel the stars with you, and visit all these new locations with you – but... well, one thing at a time.

Nykke relaxes her body against you, pulling your cheek up against her firm right boob, as she idly fantasizes about leaving Uveto with you and traveling with you on your adventures. The two of you would travel to these ‘hot’ places; she could fly over the tops of the ‘jungles’ and she would help you navigate the dark ‘caves,’ and then, when you two find a spot that isn’t too warm, or too cold, or too jungle or too cave, she finds the perfect lair, one as big as could be, where you and her can raise your own clutch.

Of course, at the first thought of having her own clutch, she can’t help but get carried away with herself. Every time she takes a breath through her nose, your scent sharply defines yourself in her fantasies, and in those fantasies, you and she have settled and you’ve gotten started on your clutch. A huge one. One that fills the jungles and the caves until they’re full of nothing but your clutch; you two would find a world that has no Frostwyrms in it, and you’d spread your kind until you are the dominant species.

Then, she, in her fantasy, and projected to you through your psionic connection, looks right into your eyes. I know you can see all this, she says to you. It would take time... but we could make all this real, [pc.name].

Her psionic projection of herself grabs you by the hand and takes you to the mouth of your cave, overlooking an expansive jungle, reaching as far into the horizon as any ocean. Imagine it, she says, her hand waving over your visage of the treetops. An entire world, dominated by our progeny. And once our kips establish themselves as the apex predator of one world, we go and propagate the next.
Her body turns towards you, and her Frostwyrmling cock is as erect as could possibly be, bobbing
between your bodies, dripping its pre to the ground
right between your eyes, dripping its pre onto your nose
above your head, dripping its pre onto your [pc.hair]
. Do not pretend you would not enjoy every last second of it, either.

The psionic vision ends abruptly, and you’re back on cold, unforgiving Uveto, sitting in Nykke’s tiny makeshift lair. Her legs and thighs shift against each other, and you can see the beginnings of a half-chub peek from between her thick, muscular thighs. She makes no effort to hide it – after all, you two are mates. You should see what she sees and want what she wants.
Well... it’s working.

Lying as you are next to Nykke, you snake your hand down one of her large, muscular arms, until your hand meets hers. You intermingle your fingers with her claws, and give her hand an affectionate squeeze. Nykke recognizes the action – she probably learned it from her Ausar Qal – and she squeezes back, although you can sense the tentativeness; she must suspect that you’re going to say something.
You tell Nykke that you’d like to talk to her about something, but she doesn’t need to answer if she isn’t ready. You recognize that it’s a bit of a sensitive topic for her.
“It’s about my scales, isn’t it?” she asks defensively. You respond affirmatively.

Nykke says nothing further, and instead lets out a long, heavy sigh, in through her nose and out through her mouth. With the three arms you’re not holding, she lifts her hands to her eyes, so she can get a closer look at her own body. Her onyx-black scales absorb what little natural light her little lair gets, making it easy to focus on her compared to the glassy blues of the ice surrounding you both.

original: “Frostwyrms are a very emotional kind,” she says. “I, uh... it was tough for me, to look at my Qim and wonder why I was so different from them. The first few Sky Cycles, it was hard for me not to look at my reflection in the ocean and not consider myself a f-... a freak.”

Nykke takes another, longer, deeper sigh through her nose. She’s mastered her psionics, but... perhaps because of her half-Ausar upbringing, you can feel her control on herself slipping. You can feel a sort of cutting pain in your heart – it’s very slight, but if you’re feeling it, you can only imagine what Nykke has been suppressing for the last two hundred years.

“I envy [frostwyrm.name], you know,” Nykke says, almost curtly. “We both couldn’t find a mate for over two-hundred Sky Cycles, but she found you first. You had given her a clutch before I had even known what it’s like to be held in someone else’s embrace. She has her own lair, and it’s huge.” She hesitates before continuing; you can hear the lilt in her voice as she goes on, reaching her breaking point. “But above all... she’s a real Frostwyrm. Pure-blooded. She’s....”

[frostwyrm.name] sometimes likes to remind you that you weren’t her first choice in mate. She had spent much of her life searching for another Frostwyrm like herself, and much like Nykke had done, but in all her searching, she couldn’t find one, and she had to ‘settle’ for you.

But, you continue, she also likes to remind you that she doesn’t regret her choice in the very least. If she could go back in time, and she knew everything she knows now, and she found a pure-blooded Frostwyrm to mate with, she’d choose to mate with you, every single time.

original: - you always love hearing about starships -
as well as her crew, and her job as skipper of her own ship.

“Her name is Inexorable. She’s an old girl, but still a magnificent example of a starship,” she states proudly. “We rescued her from the shipbreakers about ten years ago. In fact, when I started restoring her I didn’t even intend to make her space worthy again, merely to refurbish her and perhaps turn her into a museum ship. Eight hundred thousand tonnes of hulk is a lot to repair, after all.”
What changed, you ask.

“My husband,” a fond smile spreads across her scarlet lips as she caresses her wedding ring, “he knew how important this was to me, so he went out of his way to gather more people to join and more credits to fund it all - far more than I could have hoped for. It still took two years and millions of man-hours to get her flying again.”

“When we first started, I used my contacts and pulled favors to get a mostly former Navy crew; today it’s almost entirely civilian. Back then, I needed competent and trustworthy people who already knew what to do. At first, we were understaffed, overworked and taking every job we could get just to pay off the bank loans.”

“I was already recruiting new members since the beginning - almost a thousand a year - but it took time to train them. These people usually aren’t permanent as well, they stay for a couple years. New children join the family and the older ones move out; I don’t expect anyone to remain forever, however a few choose to stay. Though whether they decide to leave or not, I love every single one just as much.”
How many people, you ask, does she have on her ship right now.
“Over three thousand two hundred officers and crew,” she says with obvious pride.
You blink. “Why so many?”

She chuckles at your reaction. “Because I choose to employ minimal automation on Inexorable. I want those onboard to truly feel responsible for the ship’s wellbeing, and it allows more people to join in on our adventure. All are welcome aboard my ship as long as they’re willing to learn.”
When she says minimal automation, you ask, does that mean she has them do everything by hand?

“Yes, it does. Almost everything from mopping the deck, food preparation, shoe repair and tailoring to carrying laundry from their berths to the laundromat. I think highly of my crew so I know they can handle it.” She smiles dotingly. “Like most children, they tend to complain about the chores. But when VIPs come aboard, and they put on their dress uniforms and stand at attention to welcome them, knowing that the ship is in pristine condition due to their own hard work, the pride they feel is indescribable.”
You lean over, resting your elbows on the table, and ask her why she’s on this backwater ice ball.

She sets her drink down and smiles, appreciative of your interest. “This is where we replenish our savicite every few months of travel. Actually, this is the first time we’ve been back since we set out on the Planet Rush.”
You chime in, asking why come all the way here when she could refuel at most other stations.

“My ship has a very high operating cost, so if we can save a few million credits by skipping the distributor and buying straight from the refinery, we’ll do so. But this time we ran into a problem.” You raise an eyebrow. “You see, our arrival here was... unexpected, so there weren’t any shipments of savicite reserved for us, and it would be unwise to leave orbit until we get something. Burning deuterium and helium-3 alone might not be enough, so I’m down here right now with a legal team to negotiate with Steele Industries for an emergency resupply. We need just enough to make it to Tavros Station.”
